description: A vertical, designed Department of Labor graphic combining a photo, a slogan, and an embedded bar chart. The top is a color photograph of President Trump standing outdoors against blurred green foliage, in a dark navy suit, white shirt, and pink-red tie, arms spread wide in a triumphant 'told you so' gesture. Beneath him, in large italic white serif capitals, the slogan reads 'THE PANICANS WERE WRONG AGAIN!' Below that, on a white rounded card, is a two-bar chart titled in navy 'Job Growth in April Nearly Doubles Expectations Under President Trump,' subtitled 'Change in nonfarm employment, thousands.' A tall red bar labeled '+115k' (Actual) towers over a shorter navy bar labeled '+65k' (Expected), with a y-axis from 0 to 140 and a footnote 'Note: CEA analysis of BLS data.' A small white DOL/eagle emblem sits at the bottom. The technique is a victory-lap / 'the doubters were wrong' meme using the Trump-coined epithet 'PANICANS,' the triumphant pose, and a favorable actual-vs-expected statistic to tout strong jobs numbers. Target audience: supporters and economic-news watchers, framing critics as repeatedly wrong about the economy.
notable_text: THE PANICANS WERE WRONG AGAIN!; Job Growth in April Nearly Doubles Expectations Under President Trump; Actual +115k vs Expected +65k; CEA analysis of BLS data (tweet: TRUST IN TRUMP!)
